title: The Silk Road
topic: How trade routes connected China, Central Asia, and the Mediterranean from roughly 130 BCE onward.

steps.note: Wide regional map. Teacher reads the introductory paragraph while this state holds.

steps.label: China
steps.note: Anchors the eastern end of the route as the teacher names it.

steps.label: Roman Empire
steps.note: Western anchor. Teacher names Rome as the destination market for silk.

steps.label: The Silk Road
steps.note: Long trace gives the teacher time to describe the journey. Linear motion; do not ease.
What flowed along the route. Symbolic, not pictorial. No images of goods.

steps.event: Han dynasty formally opens the route
steps.note: Locates the route in time. First marker on this page's timeline.
Shows the duration of the system, not just its start.
